patch.value: Build package-first pm ecosystem and install command

Rename the pm extension ecosystem toward packages, restore pm install as the user-facing package installer, and model Pi-style packages that can bundle extensions, SDK integrations, prompts, skills, themes, and docs.

Pi package model researched and documented in tracker; pm package/install plan decomposed into child work; pm install can install managed package sources while preserving extension compatibility; SDK/docs/tests updated; full CLI/SDK dogfood passes in a temporary project; telemetry and Sentry verification are recorded.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Duplicate check before create: ran pm context --limit 10,pm search packages extensions SDK install --limit 10,pm list-open --limit 20,and pm list-in-progress --limit 20. Existing matches were closed extension/SDK/install work plus open parent pm-rnpb; no active item covered package-first ecosystem rename and pm install restoration,so this child feature is net-new under pm-rnpb.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Pi package research snapshot 2026-05-11: pi.dev/packages advertises npm-published package catalog entries and installs with pi install npm:<package>. Current Pi docs define packages as bundles of extensions, skills, prompt templates, and themes; package.json pi manifest supports extensions/skills/prompts/themes arrays plus gallery image/video; conventional directories are auto-discovered if no pi manifest exists. Sources include npm, git URLs/shorthands, and local paths; project-local installs use -l and persist to .pi/settings.json; temporary try-run uses -e. Pi package manager dedupes project over global, supports filtering per resource type, pins versioned npm/git refs, runs npm install for npm/git packages, and loads resources through a central resource-loader.

patch.value:
Package ecosystem plan after Pi research:

1. pm-9x1c in progress: ship package-first command aliases and pm install, including local package roots, GitHub package roots, npm:<spec> package roots, pm.extensions/pi.extensions, conventional extension directories, contracts, native Pi, MCP, docs, and tests.
2. pm-t5ud open: design the full package manifest/resource model for extensions, skills, prompts, themes, docs, commands, and SDK resources.
3. pm-su6i open: implement package resource loading beyond extension manifests, with project/global precedence and diagnostics.
4. pm-lwun open: complete package-first terminology migration in help, docs, skills, examples, telemetry naming, and UX while keeping pm extension compatibility.
5. pm-2b3l open: define marketplace/gallery metadata and package discovery, informed by pi.dev/packages npm keyword/catalog behavior.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Implementation slice completed: child pm-9x1c closed after package-first aliases, pm install, package-root/npm install support, SDK/Pi/MCP contracts, docs/skills, manual temp-project dogfood, full release gates, and observability checks. Roadmap children remain open for the broader Pi-style package ecosystem: manifest/resource model (pm-t5ud), resource loader for skills/prompts/themes/docs (pm-su6i), package gallery metadata (pm-2b3l), and full terminology migration (pm-lwun).

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Execution start 2026-05-11 package-core refactor continuation: bootstrap completed with npm install -g ., pm --version 2026.5.11, node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.0, and pnpm build. Duplicate check reused pm-59gj plus children pm-t5ud, pm-su6i, pm-lwun, pm-2b3l instead of creating a duplicate. Sub-agents are mapping CLI core, SDK/extensions, specialized features, and verification surfaces before implementation.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: External research refresh 2026-05-11: Pi packages bundle extensions, skills, prompts, and themes; support npm/git/local sources; run npm install for npm/git packages; use package.json pi manifests or convention directories; support per-resource filters, project-over-global dedupe, enable/disable controls, and explicit security warnings. OpenClaw separates small core from npm/community plugins, supports local/archive/npm/marketplace installs, list/inspect/doctor/enable/disable, discovery precedence, plugin slots, manifest-declared capabilities, and focused SDK subpath imports. Implication for pm: keep item/storage/history/config/output/package loader primitives in core; move optional workflows/resources/integrations behind installable packages with manifest contracts and diagnostics.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Scope correction 2026-05-11: user clarified Pi/OpenClaw were architecture references only. Main pm CLI/SDK must not contain direct Pi agent integration or agent-bundle resource kinds such as prompts/skills/MCPs. Pivoting implementation to remove direct Pi coupling from core code and keep packages focused on general project-management extensions/resources.

value.author: codex-agent
value.text: Evidence update after user scope correction: direct Pi references were removed from source/package/docs/tests; package manifest support was narrowed to pm.extensions only; provider-compatible tool schema is now provider-neutral (PM_PROVIDER_TOOL_PARAMETERS_SCHEMA). Focused build and tests passed: pnpm build; node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- package-manifest/sdk-index/extension-command/extension-command-github/todos/builtin-entrypoints/main-loose-options/claude-plugin-contract/release-readiness-runtime.
